entities.name: BRACHIARIA HUMIDICOLA
entities.description: A tropical pasture grass found to have nitrification inhibitory activity in its root tissues

entities.name: NITRIFICATION INHIBITORY ACTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a substance to inhibit the process of nitrification

entities.name: ACTIVITY-GUIDED FRACTIONATION
entities.description: A technique used to isolate active compounds based on their biological activity

entities.name: NITROSOMONAS EUROPAEA
entities.description: A bacterium used in a recombinant form to detect nitrification inhibitory activity

entities.name: LUXAB GENES
entities.description: Bioluminescent genes derived from Vibrio harveyi and inserted into Nitrosomonas europaea

entities.name: METHYL-P-COUMARATE
entities.description: An active nitrification inhibitory compound isolated from Brachiaria humidicola

entities.name: METHYL FERULATE
entities.description: An active nitrification inhibitory compound isolated from Brachiaria humidicola

entities.name: P-COUMARIC ACID
entities.description: The free acid form of methyl-p-coumarate which does not show inhibitory activity

entities.name: FERULIC ACID
entities.description: The free acid form of methyl ferulate which does not show inhibitory activity

entities.name: NITRIFICATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting ammonia to nitrate

entities.name: IC50
entities.description: The half maximal inhibitory concentration used to quantify the potency of inhibitors
